title: Slow Simmered Italian Burrata Tiramisu
description: A low-calorie Italian dessert featuring burrata cheese, slow simmered in a copper pot and formed into a tiramisu shape, with a nutty and juicy flavor profile reminiscent of Puglian cuisine. This solo dinner dish is perfect for a quiet evening at home.
instructions: Preheat the copper pot over low heat, adding the unsalted butter to melt and coat the bottom.
Add the chopped hazelnuts to the copper pot and toast for 5 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add the espresso, granulated sugar, cinnamon, and vanilla extract to the copper pot, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove the copper pot from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Cut the burrata cheese into small pieces and add it to the copper pot, stirring to combine with the simmered mixture.
Dip each ladyfinger into the mixture for 3-5 seconds on each side, then layer them in a large serving dish.
Repeat the layering process until all ingredients are used, finishing with a layer of burrata cheese on top.
Refrigerate the tiramisu for at least 3 hours before serving.
